exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
exercises.simplyPut: Improve your range of motion with 90/90 Hip Stretch. This beginner-level stretch targets your hips, helping to reduce stiffness, prevent injury, and promote recovery.

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Yoga/stretching reduces cortisol by 15-25% (Pascoe 2017)
Slow stretching activates vagus nerve, increasing HRV
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers
Consistent stretching increases ROM 10-30% over 4-8 weeks
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach restricts movement in forward folds. Carbonation causes discomfort in inversions.

during_workout.why_avoid: Yoga and stretching require empty or near-empty stomach for comfort, especially inversions.

post_workout.recovery_note: Anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, berries, omega-3) support flexibility gains and reduce DOMS.

foodsPositive.benefit: Collagen peptides support connective tissue elasticity
foodsPositive.mechanism: Glycine and proline provide building blocks for collagen cross-linking in tendons/fascia

foodsPositive.benefit: ALA omega-3 supports joint lubrication
foodsPositive.mechanism: Alpha-linolenic acid converts to EPA, reducing arachidonic acid-driven inflammation

foodsPositive.benefit: Vitamin C essential for collagen synthesis
foodsPositive.mechanism: Ascorbic acid is cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase, required for collagen triple helix formation
foodsPositive.dose: 1 medium red pepper (169mg vitamin C = 188% DV)

foodsPositive.benefit: Bromelain enzyme reduces tissue swelling post-stretch
foodsPositive.mechanism: Proteolytic enzyme breaks down fibrin, reducing edema and improving tissue drainage

foodsPositive.benefit: Curcumin reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility
foodsPositive.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB pathway, reducing TNF-α and IL-1β in synovial fluid

foodsNegative.impairment: Dulls pain perception — risk of overstretching and tearing
foodsNegative.mechanism: Alcohol suppresses nociceptors, masking tissue damage signals
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Muscle relaxants + alcohol = excessive CNS depression

foodsNegative.impairment: Increases muscle tension, counteracting stretching goals
foodsNegative.mechanism: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, maintaining motor neuron excitability
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Fluoroquinolone antibiotics + caffeine = tendon damage risk

foodsNegative.impairment: Fascia becomes less pliable when dehydrated
foodsNegative.mechanism: Hyaluronic acid in fascia requires hydration for viscoelastic properties
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Diuretics compound dehydration — stretch with extra caution

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Yoga/stretching reduces cortisol by 15-25% (Pascoe 2017)
Slow stretching activates vagus nerve, increasing HRV
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach restricts movement in forward folds. Carbonation causes discomfort in inversions.

during_workout.why_avoid: Yoga and stretching require empty or near-empty stomach for comfort, especially inversions.

post_workout.recovery_note: Anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, berries, omega-3) support flexibility gains and reduce DOMS.

foodsPositive.benefit: Collagen peptides support connective tissue elasticity
foodsPositive.mechanism: Glycine and proline provide building blocks for collagen cross-linking in tendons/fascia

foodsPositive.benefit: ALA omega-3 supports joint lubrication
foodsPositive.mechanism: Alpha-linolenic acid converts to EPA, reducing arachidonic acid-driven inflammation

foodsPositive.benefit: Curcumin reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility
foodsPositive.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB pathway, reducing TNF-α and IL-1β in synovial fluid

foodsPositive.benefit: Vitamin C essential for collagen synthesis
foodsPositive.mechanism: Ascorbic acid is cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase, required for collagen triple helix formation
foodsPositive.dose: 1 medium red pepper (169mg vitamin C = 188% DV)

foodsNegative.impairment: Dulls pain perception — risk of overstretching and tearing
foodsNegative.mechanism: Alcohol suppresses nociceptors, masking tissue damage signals
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Muscle relaxants + alcohol = excessive CNS depression

foodsNegative.impairment: Increases muscle tension, counteracting stretching goals
foodsNegative.mechanism: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, maintaining motor neuron excitability
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Fluoroquinolone antibiotics + caffeine = tendon damage risk

foodsNegative.impairment: Fascia becomes less pliable when dehydrated
foodsNegative.mechanism: Hyaluronic acid in fascia requires hydration for viscoelastic properties
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Diuretics compound dehydration — stretch with extra caution

post_workout.recovery_note: Anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, berries, omega-3) support flexibility gains and reduce DOMS.

foodsPositive.benefit: ALA omega-3 supports joint lubrication
foodsPositive.mechanism: Alpha-linolenic acid converts to EPA, reducing arachidonic acid-driven inflammation

foodsPositive.benefit: Collagen peptides support connective tissue elasticity
foodsPositive.mechanism: Glycine and proline provide building blocks for collagen cross-linking in tendons/fascia

foodsPositive.benefit: Curcumin reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility
foodsPositive.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB pathway, reducing TNF-α and IL-1β in synovial fluid

foodsPositive.benefit: Bromelain enzyme reduces tissue swelling post-stretch
foodsPositive.mechanism: Proteolytic enzyme breaks down fibrin, reducing edema and improving tissue drainage

foodsPositive.benefit: Vitamin C essential for collagen synthesis
foodsPositive.mechanism: Ascorbic acid is cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase, required for collagen triple helix formation
foodsPositive.dose: 1 medium red pepper (169mg vitamin C = 188% DV)

foodsNegative.impairment: Increases muscle tension, counteracting stretching goals
foodsNegative.mechanism: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, maintaining motor neuron excitability
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Fluoroquinolone antibiotics + caffeine = tendon damage risk

foodsNegative.impairment: Dulls pain perception — risk of overstretching and tearing
foodsNegative.mechanism: Alcohol suppresses nociceptors, masking tissue damage signals
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Muscle relaxants + alcohol = excessive CNS depression

foodsNegative.impairment: Fascia becomes less pliable when dehydrated
foodsNegative.mechanism: Hyaluronic acid in fascia requires hydration for viscoelastic properties
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Diuretics compound dehydration — stretch with extra caution

exercises.name: Basketball Cool-Down: Foam Roll 10Min
exercises.simplyPut: Improve your range of motion with Basketball Cool-Down: Foam Roll 10Min. This beginner-level stretch targets your full body, helping to reduce stiffness, prevent injury, and promote recovery.

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Consistent stretching increases ROM 10-30% over 4-8 weeks
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers
Yoga/stretching reduces cortisol by 15-25% (Pascoe 2017)
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg
Slow stretching activates vagus nerve, increasing HRV

exercises.name: Downward-Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana)
exercises.simplyPut: Form an inverted V-shape with your body — hands and feet on the floor, hips pushed high. This is the most iconic yoga pose and one of the most effective full-body stretches. It lengthens your entire posterior chain while building shoulder and arm strength.
exercises.howTo:
1. Start on hands and knees (tabletop position)
2. Hands shoulder-width, fingers spread wide
3. Tuck toes, lift knees off floor
4. Push hips up and back toward ceiling
5. Straighten legs as much as possible (slight knee bend is OK)
6. Push chest toward thighs
7. Heels reach toward floor (they don't need to touch)
8. Head between arms, ears in line with upper arms
9. Hold for 5-10 breaths, pressing evenly through hands

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

foodsNegative.mechanism: Alcohol suppresses nociceptors, masking tissue damage signals
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Muscle relaxants + alcohol = excessive CNS depression

exercises.name: Foam Roll: Mid Back
exercises.simplyPut: Improve your range of motion with Foam Roll: Mid Back. This beginner-level stretch targets your mid back, helping to reduce stiffness, prevent injury, and promote recovery.

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Yoga/stretching reduces cortisol by 15-25% (Pascoe 2017)
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg
Consistent stretching increases ROM 10-30% over 4-8 weeks
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers
Slow stretching activates vagus nerve, increasing HRV

exercises.name: Foam Roll: Quadriceps
exercises.simplyPut: Lie face down with a foam roller under your thighs and roll back and forth, pausing on tender spots. Foam rolling is like giving yourself a deep tissue massage — it breaks up muscle knots (trigger points), improves blood flow, and reduces soreness after tough workouts.
exercises.howTo: Perform Foam Roll: Quadriceps with controlled breathing and gradual range of motion.

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Slow stretching activates vagus nerve, increasing HRV
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg
Consistent stretching increases ROM 10-30% over 4-8 weeks

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach restricts movement in forward folds. Carbonation causes discomfort in inversions.

during_workout.why_avoid: Yoga and stretching require empty or near-empty stomach for comfort, especially inversions.

post_workout.recovery_note: Anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, berries, omega-3) support flexibility gains and reduce DOMS.

foodsPositive.benefit: ALA omega-3 supports joint lubrication
foodsPositive.mechanism: Alpha-linolenic acid converts to EPA, reducing arachidonic acid-driven inflammation

foodsPositive.benefit: Vitamin C essential for collagen synthesis
foodsPositive.mechanism: Ascorbic acid is cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase, required for collagen triple helix formation
foodsPositive.dose: 1 medium red pepper (169mg vitamin C = 188% DV)

foodsPositive.benefit: Curcumin reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility
foodsPositive.mechanism: Inhibits NF-κB pathway, reducing TNF-α and IL-1β in synovial fluid

foodsPositive.benefit: Bromelain enzyme reduces tissue swelling post-stretch
foodsPositive.mechanism: Proteolytic enzyme breaks down fibrin, reducing edema and improving tissue drainage

foodsNegative.impairment: Increases muscle tension, counteracting stretching goals
foodsNegative.mechanism: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors, maintaining motor neuron excitability
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Fluoroquinolone antibiotics + caffeine = tendon damage risk

foodsNegative.impairment: Fascia becomes less pliable when dehydrated
foodsNegative.mechanism: Hyaluronic acid in fascia requires hydration for viscoelastic properties
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Diuretics compound dehydration — stretch with extra caution

foodsNegative.impairment: Dulls pain perception — risk of overstretching and tearing
foodsNegative.mechanism: Alcohol suppresses nociceptors, masking tissue damage signals
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Muscle relaxants + alcohol = excessive CNS depression

exercises.name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
exercises.simplyPut: Stand on one leg and place the other heel on a raised surface, then lean forward until you feel a stretch in the back of your thigh. Tight hamstrings cause back pain, limit athletic performance, and make bending over feel like a chore. This simple stretch fixes all of that.

exercises.name: Sun Salutation A (Surya Namaskar)
exercises.simplyPut: A flowing sequence of 12 poses that takes you from standing, to the floor, through a backbend, and back to standing. It's like a full-body warm-up disguised as a prayer to the sun. Yogis have done this sequence for thousands of years.
exercises.howTo:
1. Mountain Pose (Tadasana): Stand tall, hands at heart
2. Upward Salute (Urdhva Hastasana): Reach arms overhead, slight backbend
3. Forward Fold (Uttanasana): Hinge at hips, fold forward
4. Half Lift (Ardha Uttanasana): Flat back, hands on shins
5. Plank Pose: Step or jump back to high plank
6. Chaturanga: Lower halfway down (like a slow push-up)
7. Upward-Facing Dog (Urdhva Mukha Svanasana): Press up, open chest
8. Downward-Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana): Lift hips up and back
9. Hold Downward Dog for 5 breaths
10. Step or jump feet to hands
11. Forward Fold → Half Lift → Forward Fold
12. Rise to Upward Salute → Mountain Pose

exercises.name: Tree Pose (Vrksasana)
exercises.simplyPut: Stand on one foot with the other foot pressed against your inner thigh, hands at heart or overhead. Tree pose teaches balance, focus, and patience. Like a tree, you need strong roots (standing foot) and a steady trunk (core) to stand tall in the wind.

exercises.name: Warrior II (Virabhadrasana II)
exercises.simplyPut: Stand in a wide stance with one knee bent, arms extended in both directions, and gaze over your front hand. Warrior II opens your hips and builds endurance in your legs. It's the pose of presence — you're here, you're steady, you're ready.
exercises.howTo: Perform Warrior II (Virabhadrasana II) with controlled breathing and gradual range of motion.

exercises.name: Yoga Sun Salutation B (Beginner)
exercises.simplyPut: Improve your range of motion with Yoga Sun Salutation B (Beginner). This beginner-level stretch targets your full body, helping to reduce stiffness, prevent injury, and promote recovery.

mistakes.correction: Dynamic warm-up first; save static stretching for after warm-up or post-workout
Mild discomfort OK; sharp or stabbing pain = back off immediately

mistakes.consequence: Increased muscle tension (fight/flight response), reduced ROM
mistakes.correction: Slow exhale while deepening stretch; breathing drives relaxation response
5-10 min light cardio warm-up before stretching; or stretch post-workout

mistakes.consequence: Joint instability, chronic pain, ligament laxity
mistakes.correction: If Beighton score >4: focus on strengthening in mid-range, not increasing ROM

physiologyLinks.effect: Yoga/stretching reduces cortisol by 15-25% (Pascoe 2017)
Regular stretching reduces arterial stiffness and systolic BP by 2-5 mmHg
Consistent stretching increases ROM 10-30% over 4-8 weeks
Regular yoga practice reduces CRP and IL-6 markers

during_workout.why_avoid: Yoga and stretching require empty or near-empty stomach for comfort, especially inversions.

post_workout.recovery_note: Anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, berries, omega-3) support flexibility gains and reduce DOMS.
